column2: Five office PCs need to share resources without centralized authentication. Each PC will maintain its own user accounts. Should the technician configure a domain or a workgroup?
column5: Workgroups fit small peer networks because account and device administration remains on each computer. A domain is the better match when centralized authentication and management are required.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 13
column10: A+::220-1202::1.7 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsNetworkingFeatures Basic HighYield WindowsNetworking Workgroup Source::Messer-v140

column2: An organization wants users to authenticate centrally and access managed resources from many Windows clients. Should the clients join a domain or a workgroup?
column5: A domain centralizes authentication and device access across a business network. Joining it also requires an eligible Windows edition and appropriate rights.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 13
column10: A+::220-1202::1.7 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsNetworkingFeatures Basic HighYield WindowsNetworking Domain Source::Messer-v140

column2: A user repeatedly accesses a file-server share and wants it to appear in File Explorer with a local drive letter. What should the technician configure?
column5: Mapping associates a drive letter with a server share and can reconnect it automatically. Entering the network path directly reaches the same resource without creating the drive-letter association.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, pp. 13 and 15
column10: A+::220-1202::1.7 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsNetworkingFeatures Basic HighYield WindowsNetworking FileSharing MappedDrive Source::Messer-v140

column2: A printer is connected to one Windows PC and must be made available to other network users. Which property should the technician configure on the host PC?
column5: Windows shares an existing printer through Printer Properties. Other users can then add the shared printer through File Explorer or Windows Settings.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 13
column10: A+::220-1202::1.7 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsNetworkingFeatures Basic HighYield WindowsNetworking PrinterSharing Source::Messer-v140

column2: A technician wants to open the Projects share on a server named File01 directly in File Explorer. What network path should be entered?
column5: A Windows network path identifies the server first and the share second. The same path can later be assigned a drive letter if persistent mapped access is useful.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 15
column10: A+::220-1202::1.7 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsNetworkingFeatures Basic WindowsNetworking FileSharing NetworkPath Source::Messer-v140

column2: An approved application needs inbound network access, but the Windows firewall must remain enabled. What should the technician configure?
column4: An application or feature exception in Windows Defender Firewall.
column5: A targeted exception permits the approved application without removing host firewall protection for unrelated traffic. Disabling the entire firewall is a broader and less secure change.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 14
column10: A+::220-1202::1.7 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsNetworkingFeatures Basic HighYield WindowsNetworking Firewall ApplicationException Source::Messer-v140

column2: A Windows client already has allowed-app exceptions, but the current network requires the most restrictive inbound setting. Which firewall option ignores the exception list?
column5: This setting overrides ordinary allowed-app exceptions for the applicable network profile. It is a deliberate restrictive mode, not the normal way to permit a trusted application.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 14
column10: A+::220-1202::1.7 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsNetworkingFeatures Basic WindowsNetworking Firewall Source::Messer-v140

column2: No predefined firewall exception matches a required inbound port. Which type of Windows Defender Firewall rule gives the technician control over that port?
column5: Predefined exceptions cover common cases. A custom rule is appropriate when the technician must explicitly allow or block a port or apply other detailed conditions.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 14
column10: A+::220-1202::1.7 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsNetworkingFeatures Basic WindowsNetworking Firewall CustomRule Source::Messer-v140

column2: A Windows client should receive its IP configuration automatically from the network. Should the adapter use static addressing or DHCP?
column5: DHCP supplies dynamic client configuration automatically. Static configuration instead requires the technician to enter the necessary IP parameters.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 14
column10: A+::220-1202::1.7 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsNetworkingFeatures Basic HighYield WindowsNetworking DHCP DynamicAddressing Source::Messer-v140

column2: A Windows client must always use specific administrator-provided IP settings instead of requesting them automatically. Which addressing method should be configured?
column5: Static configuration manually fixes the client's IP parameters. The technician must supply correct values for the local network rather than rely on DHCP.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 14
column10: A+::220-1202::1.7 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsNetworkingFeatures Basic WindowsNetworking StaticAddressing Source::Messer-v140

column2: A Windows client configured for automatic addressing receives a 169.254.x.x address and cannot reach the internet. What addressing fallback is the client using?
column5: Windows uses Automatic Private IP Addressing when it has no static address and cannot obtain DHCP configuration. The link-local address may support nearby communication but does not provide normal routed connectivity.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 14
column10: A+::220-1202::1.7 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsNetworkingFeatures Basic HighYield WindowsNetworking APIPA DHCP Source::Messer-v140

column2: A technician is entering a static IPv4 configuration. Which value tells the client which destination addresses are on its local subnet?
column5: The mask defines the local subnet boundary. The default gateway has a different role: it is used when the destination lies outside that local subnet.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 14
column10: A+::220-1202::1.7 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsNetworkingFeatures Basic HighYield WindowsNetworking SubnetMask Source::Messer-v140

column2: A Windows client can communicate with hosts on its local subnet but cannot reach any off-subnet IP address. Which client setting should be checked?
column5: The default gateway routes traffic beyond the local subnet. Because the scenario already confirms local IP communication, the subnet's immediate link is working and the gateway setting is the relevant client value.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 14
column10: A+::220-1202::1.7 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsNetworkingFeatures Basic HighYield WindowsNetworking DefaultGateway Source::Messer-v140

column2: A Windows client reaches a server by IP address but not by hostname. Which client network setting should the technician check?
column5: Domain Name System settings identify the resolver used to translate names into IP addresses. Successful access by IP narrows this scenario to name resolution rather than general routing.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 14
column10: A+::220-1202::1.7 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsNetworkingFeatures Basic HighYield WindowsNetworking DNS Source::Messer-v140

column2: A mobile Windows client normally uses DHCP, but it needs known static settings when connected to a network where DHCP is unavailable. What adapter feature should the technician configure?
column5: Alternate Configuration supplies defined fallback values when DHCP cannot be reached. Without a configured static fallback, Windows normally uses APIPA.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 14
column10: A+::220-1202::1.7 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsNetworkingFeatures Basic WindowsNetworking AlternateConfiguration DHCP Source::Messer-v140

column2: A remote employee needs to use the built-in Windows client to connect to the workplace network with assigned credentials. Which network connection should the technician configure?
column5: Windows includes a VPN client for workplace connections. Depending on the organization, authentication may use entered credentials or a smart card.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 14
column10: A+::220-1202::1.7 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsNetworkingFeatures Basic HighYield WindowsNetworking VPN Source::Messer-v140

column2: A laptop must connect through a mobile carrier when neither Ethernet nor Wi-Fi is the intended access method. Which Windows network connection type applies?
column5: Wireless wide area networking uses compatible cellular hardware and carrier service. Provider-specific software and setup can vary, so confirm the adapter and service requirements.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 14
column10: A+::220-1202::1.7 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsNetworkingFeatures Basic WindowsNetworking WWAN Cellular Source::Messer-v140

column2: An organization requires supported client traffic to pass through an intermediary address, except for specified internal destinations. Which Windows network setting should be configured?
column4: Proxy settings, including the proxy address and bypass exceptions.
column5: A proxy changes the path used by supported application traffic. Exceptions identify destinations that should bypass it, and not every application or protocol necessarily uses the same proxy configuration.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 15
column10: A+::220-1202::1.7 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsNetworkingFeatures Basic WindowsNetworking Proxy Source::Messer-v140

column2: A user connects a Windows laptop to untrusted café Wi-Fi and does not need local device sharing. Should the technician select the public or private network profile?
column5: Public is the restrictive choice for an untrusted network. Private is intended for a trusted home or work network where local discovery and sharing are needed; firewall behavior can differ by profile.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, pp. 14-15
column10: A+::220-1202::1.7 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsNetworkingFeatures Basic HighYield WindowsNetworking NetworkProfile PublicNetwork Source::Messer-v140

column2: A Windows client is using a usage-billed link. Which connection setting tells Windows and supported applications to reduce some background data use?
column5: Metering is useful for capped, costly, or slow links and can reduce activities such as updates or synchronization. It does not correct invalid IP, DNS, or gateway settings.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 15
column10: A+::220-1202::1.7 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsNetworkingFeatures Basic HighYield WindowsNetworking MeteredConnection Source::Messer-v140
